senses.definition: Any member of the order Siphonophorae, a colonial marine animal that functions as a single organism made up of several specialized zooids.
examples: The Portuguese man-of-war is a famous siphonophore.
Researchers study siphonophores to understand how colonies coordinate.

senses.definition: Plural form referring to multiple organisms of the siphonophore group.
examples: Siphonophores can form long, delicate chains in the open sea.
Different siphonophores have different specialized roles within their colony.

senses.definition: Describing something related to siphonophores or resembling a siphonophore.
examples: The siphonophorous organism displayed the characteristic segmented body.
A siphonophorous structure typical of hydrozoan colonies.

senses.definition: A taxonomic name likely referring to a genus or group within a particular paleobiological or invertebrate context.
examples: Siphonoplax fossils have been studied to understand ancient predatory lifestyles.

senses.definition: A structural opening or pore in siphonophore anatomy, typically part of their colonial zooid arrangement.
examples: Researchers examined the siphonopore to understand nutrient uptake.
The pore serves as a passage for fluids within the colony.

senses.definition: Relating to or situated near the siphon organ and the brain region in some organisms; used in specialized anatomical terminology.
examples: The siphonorhinal region was examined in the study of the siphonophore nervous system.

senses.definition: Variant spelling related to a siphon-related anatomical term; used in specialized texts.
examples: The siphorhinal features were described in historical literature.

senses.definition: A part of a siphonophore colony that includes feeding and reproductive zooids; a body segment of the colony.
examples: The siphosome contains several specialized zooids in the siphonophore.

senses.definition: A tube-like organ in some cephalopods or related fossils used for gas exchange or buoyancy control; also used in other marine invertebrates as a structural feature.
examples: The ammonite siphuncle helped regulate buoyancy.
In some molluscs, the siphuncle adjusts internal gas pressure.

senses.definition: Having a siphuncle or provided with a siphuncle; used in anatomical descriptions.
examples: The fossil was siphuncled, indicating buoyancy adaptations.

senses.definition: Duplicate entry for siphosome; see above.
examples: The siphosome contains multiple specialized zooids.

senses.definition: A central cylindrical structure of vascular tissue in some fossil plants or primitive stems.
examples: Fossils show a siphonostele arrangement in early land plants.

senses.definition: Plural of siphonostele; central vascular structures found in certain plants.
examples: Ferns and some primitive plants exhibit siphonosteles.

senses.definition: Related to siphonostele, a central stem structure in some plants.
examples: The siphonostelic arrangement is characteristic of early vascular plants.

senses.definition: In a siphonostele manner; relating to the central stem structure in plants.
examples: The fossil displayed siphonostely preserved xylem patterns.

senses.definition: A named genus or morphological feature in certain siphonophore-related or marine organisms.
examples: Siphonostoma is cited in marine taxonomy literature.

senses.definition: A taxonomic group or order-level clade within siphonophore-related organisms; often used in paleontological or marine biology contexts.
examples: Fossil records include siphonostomata specimens.

senses.definition: Having a siphonostome or possessing siphonostomous features; used in descriptive taxonomy.
examples: The siphonostomatous shell exhibited distinctive ridges.

senses.definition: An opening or mouth-like structure in certain siphonophore organisms; also a term used for certain plant or invertebrate features.
examples: The siphonostome is a key feature in identifying this organism.

senses.definition: Having siphonostomes or siphonostomous features; used in descriptive taxonomy.
examples: The siphonostomous mouthparts were studied to determine feeding methods.

senses.definition: A specialized, non-feeding zooid in a siphonophore colony responsible for tasks such as defense or propulsion.
examples: Siphonozooids help coordinate colony movement.

senses.definition: Flexible tubes or devices that draw in or expel liquid or gas; a verb sense exists as a common action to draw off liquid.
examples: The bartender used a siphon to pour the sparkling water.
He siphoned gasoline from the tank.

senses.definition: To draw off a liquid by using a siphon.
examples: She siphoned the wine into a decanter.
They siphoned fuel from the container.

senses.definition: A taxonomic term possibly referring to a subunit or component within siphonophore anatomy.
examples: The siphonula forms part of the colony's feeding apparatus.

senses.definition: Variant or misspelled form related to siphon or nasal/anatomical terms; appears in occasional technical texts.
examples: The siphorhinal area was noted in older anatomical diagrams.

senses.definition: Rare taxonomic or descriptive term; may refer to a person or entity associated with siphonophore studies in historical texts.
examples: The siphorhinian researchers contributed early descriptions of colonial hydrozoans.

senses.definition: Duplicate entry for siphosome; see above.
examples: The siphosome houses feeding and reproductive zooids.

senses.definition: Having the form or appearance of a siphonous structure; used in botanical or zoological descriptions.
examples: The siphosed stem indicates a siphonous morphology.
